mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and corporate earnings potential, leading to positive equity returns as investors price in a stronger economy.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year inflation below 1% signals weak pricing power but also implies a continued accommodative stance from the Federal Reserve, lowering the discount rate for equities and boosting forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low (below 5.5%) while the Fed Funds rate remains near the zero lower bound, it signals a potential for future monetary policy tightening that is not yet priced in, leading to negative forward equity returns as the market anticipates higher rates.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to generate a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, strong retail sales growth signals robust demand that will force the central bank to maintain or accelerate its hiking path, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is rising, the correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in Industrial Production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and production sectors, signaling weakening corporate revenues and a higher probability of recession, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the 3-month change in INDPRO is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant quarter-over-quarter increase in housing starts reflects strong underlying consumer demand and confidence, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and positive short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST is above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (percentage of correct directional predictions) is below 55% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected month-over-month increase in the effective Federal Funds rate, even while near the zero lower bound, signals a potential shift in interbank liquidity or premature policy tightening, creating uncertainty and negative returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Market is sensitive to timing of Fed 'liftoff' from ZIRP
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below trend while inflation accelerates, it signals a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and increases uncertainty, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year GDPC1 growth < 2% and Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation of the 1970s was poor for equities
failure_condition: A portfolio that shorts the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met does not outperform a buy-and-hold strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a full economic cycle.
